why: Protocol 775 Configuration finish_configuration must dispatch from the official framed packet ids to terminal finish_configuration packet handling in both directions; terminal flags are official answer fields generated from the packet INSTANCE isTerminal() calls.
what: The Rust oracle test configuration_finish_framed_terminal_matches_official_oracle_answer panics while exercising packet::packet_by_id(775, State::Configuration, Direction::Serverbound, packet id 3, official empty body bytes) because v26_1_2.rs does not map Configuration serverbound packet id 0x03.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: ConfigurationProtocols.SERVERBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ServerboundFinishConfigurationPacket.INSTANCE), ConfigurationProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundFinishConfigurationPacket.INSTANCE), and both packet INSTANCE isTerminal() calls.
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and decoded_fields comparison surface for framed packet ids, empty bodies, and official terminal flags.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet ids from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Configuration serverbound and clientbound dispatch.
Current Protocol 775 Configuration mapping contains serverbound keep_alive id 0x04 but not finish_configuration id 0x03.
